title: Monaco 2025 mandatory two-stop pressure
summary: Add a special rule-state feature for Monaco 2025 where the race required at least two pit stops and at least three tyre sets.
rationale: External Formula 1 sources confirm the 2025 Monaco special rule, and the attached run explored this rule-state idea qualitatively. A small explicit feature can capture regulation-driven pit pressure that generic tyre windows may miss.
implementation_hint: When Race indicates Monaco and Year indicates 2025, set required pit stops to two and required tyre sets to three; otherwise use the normal conservative requirement. Derive remaining required stops from prior and current PitStop counts and interact with RaceProgress and current-tyre finish capability.
expected_effect: Small AUC gain with low-medium confidence because it affects a narrow subset, but it is high-value domain validation.
risk: Do not apply the Monaco two-stop override to other years. Hard-coding may hurt if the competition data generation does not reflect the regulation cleanly.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
